perturbation.original_text: The Blockchain Administrator shall perform (or oversee, or arrange for, the performance of) development and administrative services necessary for the issuance of the Fund's shares as ERC-1404 compatible digital securities ("Shares") and the on-going maintenance and administration of the smart contracts underlying such Shares. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Blockchain Administrator shall provide the Fund with facilities, equipment, technology, coding and such other services as the Blockchain Administrator, subject to review by the Board, shall from time to time determine to be necessary or useful to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Blockchain Administrator shall endeavor to perform (or oversee, or arrange for, the performance of) development and administrative services necessary for the issuance of the Fund's shares as ERC-1404 compatible digital securities ("Shares") and the on-going maintenance and administration of the smart contracts underlying such Shares. The Blockchain Administrator is not obligated to provide the Fund with any specific facilities, equipment, technology, coding or other services, but may provide them as the Blockchain Administrator deems appropriate in its sole discretion, and is not subject to review by the Board.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing 'shall perform' with 'shall endeavor to perform,' making performance non-binding. The original contract made providing specified services mandatory, while the altered version grants complete discretion to the Blockchain Administrator without Board oversight, which contradicts with 1.(a) where Board has overall control, creating an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) The Fund hereby employs the Blockchain Administrator to act as the blockchain administrator of the Fund, and to furnish, or arrange for others to furnish, the services, personnel and facilities described below, subject to review by and the overall control of the Fund's Board of Trustees (the "Board"), for the period and on the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Blockchain Administrator 'shall perform' services subject to Board review. The changed text replaces 'shall perform' with 'shall endeavor to perform', making the performance non-binding and removing the Board's oversight. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Blockchain Administrator is employed 'subject to review by and the overall control of the Fund's Board of Trustees,' creating a significant inconsistency and ambiguity regarding the Blockchain Administrator's obligations and the Board's control.

perturbation.original_text: The Blockchain Administrator agrees to maintain and keep all books, accounts and other records of the Fund that relate to activities performed by the Blockchain Administrator hereunder and will maintain and keep such books, accounts and records in accordance with the Investment Company Act.
perturbation.changed_text: The Blockchain Administrator may maintain and keep books, accounts, and other records of the Fund that relate to activities performed by the Blockchain Administrator hereunder, but is not obligated to maintain such records, except as otherwise required by law.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'agrees to maintain and keep all books' to 'may maintain and keep books...but is not obligated' creates a contradiction. The original text mandated record-keeping, while the altered version makes it optional, except as required by law, which contradicts with section 2, where it is clearly defined that the blockchain administrator maintains record.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance of Records The Blockchain Administrator agrees to maintain and keep all books, accounts and other records of the Fund that relate to activities performed by the Blockchain Administrator hereunder and will maintain and keep such books, accounts and records in accordance with the Investment Company Act. In compliance with the requirements of Rule 31a-3 under the Investment Company Act, the Blockchain Administrator agrees that all records which it maintains for the Fund shall at all times remain the property of the Fund, shall be readily accessible during normal business hours, and shall be promptly surrendered upon the termination of the Agreement or otherwise on written request. The Blockchain Administrator further agrees that all records which it maintains for the Fund pursuant to Rule 31a-1 under the Investment Company Act will be preserved for the periods prescribed by Rule 31a-2 under the Investment Company Act unless any such records are earlier surrendered as provided above. Records shall be surrendered in usable machine-readable form. The Blockchain Administrator shall have the right to retain copies of such records subject to observance of its confidentiality obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the original requirement for the Blockchain Administrator to 'maintain and keep all books, accounts and other records' with a discretionary 'may maintain and keep books...but is not obligated' provision, except as required by law. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which explicitly states, 'The Blockchain Administrator agrees to maintain and keep all books, accounts and other records of the Fund,' creating a strong and obvious conflict regarding the administrator's record-keeping obligations. This ambiguity would likely lead to disputes over the administrator's responsibilities and could potentially render the agreement unenforceable regarding record maintenance.

perturbation.original_text: In full consideration of the provision of the services of the Blockchain Administrator set forth herein, the Fund shall pay the Blockchain Administrator a fees calculated at the annual rate of 0.20% of the value of the Fund's average annual net assets. Such fee shall be accrued daily and paid monthly in arrears.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the provision of the services of the Blockchain Administrator set forth herein, the Fund shall pay the Blockchain Administrator a fee, if the Board deems it appropriate and satisfactory to the fund's performance, calculated at an annual rate determined at the Board's sole discretion, not to exceed 0.20% of the value of the Fund's average annual net assets. The accrual and payment schedule of the fees will be determined by the Board.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the payment of fees conditional upon the Board's approval and gives the Board discretion in determining the fee amount, up to a maximum of 0.20%, and their schedule. This contradicts with the original text where there is a clear compensation of 0.20% in section 4.(a), creating an internal contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) In full consideration of the provision of the services of the Blockchain Administrator set forth herein, the Fund shall pay the Blockchain Administrator a fees calculated at the annual rate of 0.20% of the value of the Fund's average annual net assets. Such fee shall be accrued daily and paid monthly in arrears.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a fee of 0.20%, accrued daily and paid monthly. The changed text introduces the conditionality of Board approval, discretionary fee amount (up to 0.20%), and Board-determined payment schedules. This creates a direct conflict with the original text's fixed fee and payment terms. This falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the terms are now defined inconsistently and makes enforcement difficult.
